verses.text: The people saw Moses delayed; gathered to Aaron: make us gods. Aaron took their gold earrings, fashioned a molten calf: these be thy gods, O Israel. He built an altar: tomorrow a feast to the LORD. They offered burnt and peace offerings, ate, drank, rose up to play. Guidance: Forty days felt too long. They didn't stop believing in God — they tried to fit Him into a shape they could see. Aaron called the calf the LORD; that lie is as deadly as a foreign god.

The LORD: Moses, go down — thy people whom thou broughtest out of Egypt have corrupted themselves. They turned aside quickly, made a calf, worshipped, sacrificed. A stiff-necked people. Let Me alone; My wrath shall wax hot; I will consume them; of thee will I make a great nation. Guidance: God calls them thy people now, not Mine. He offers Moses what was once offered Abraham — to father a great nation. Moses refuses the upgrade. Real leaders bind themselves to the ones who failed.

Moses besought the LORD: why doth Thy wrath wax hot against Thy people, whom Thou broughtest out with great power? Why should the Egyptians say, for mischief He brought them out? Turn from Thy fierce wrath; remember Abraham, Isaac, Israel, to whom Thou swearest by Thine own self. The LORD repented of the evil. Guidance: Moses gives God back His words — Thy people, Thy power. He pleads Egypt's reputation and the patriarchs' covenant. Prayer that grips God's name and promises moves His heart.

Moses descended with the two tables — written on both sides, work of God. Joshua: noise of war. Moses: not victory or cry — those that sing. He saw the calf and dancing; anger waxed hot. Cast the tables and brake them. Burnt the calf, ground to powder, strewed on water, made Israel drink. Guidance: Moses breaks the tables exactly where the people break the covenant. The calf burnt, ground, dissolved, swallowed — they taste the sin literally. Sin doesn't leave the body without going through it.

Moses to Aaron: what did this people do unto thee, that thou hast brought so great a sin? Aaron: let not my lord's anger wax hot. They said: make us gods. I said: who hath gold? They gave me. I cast it into the fire — and out came this calf. Guidance: Aaron blames the people, the fire, almost the calf itself. He never says: I made it. Watch how sin teaches the passive voice. Leadership owns failure even when it was the crowd's idea.

Moses saw the people naked. In the gate: who is on the LORD's side? Levi gathered. Put every man his sword — slay brother, companion, neighbor. Three thousand fell. Moses: consecrate yourselves today — even your sons and brothers — that He bestow a blessing. Guidance: A terrible day. The Levites are blessed not for tribal loyalty but for choosing God over kin when kin choose idols. The cross cuts the same way: he that loves family more than Me is not worthy.

Moses: ye have sinned greatly. I go up; peradventure I make atonement. He returned: this people sinned greatly. If Thou wilt forgive — if not, blot me out of Thy book. The LORD: whoso sinned against Me, him I blot out. Go, lead the people; Mine Angel goes before thee. The LORD plagued the people for the calf. Guidance: Moses offers to be erased in their place. Paul wrote the same wish in Romans 9. This is the heart of a mediator — Christ alone could carry it through and survive.
